title: Epidendrum steyermarkii
taxonomy: Epidendrum steyermarkii is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. This classification places Epidendrum steyermarkii within the subfamily Epidendroideae. Within the genus Epidendrum, it is likely to be classified as part of the tribe Maxillarieae. The exact classification of Epidendrum steyermarkii may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be an epiphytic orchid native to Central and South America. Further classification within the genus may require more specific information or recent taxonomic updates.

intro: Epidendrum steyermarkii, commonly known as Steyermark's Epidendrum, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Epidendrum steyermarkii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
